content.full_text:
FAIR p OLITICAL p RACTICES COMMISSION 
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 958 I 4-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Mary Lopez 
727 South Santa Fe A venue 
Vista, California 92083 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. W-11-152 
Dear Ms. Lopez: 
August 22, 2011 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest 
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act") 1 dated August 11, 2011. While the Act 
requires the Commission to provide written advice to any person whose duties under the Act are 
in question (Section 83114(b); Regulation 18329(a)), the Commission may decline to provide 
advice if a response relates to past conduct, or would be inappropriate or otherwise not in the 
public interest. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A),(F) and (c)(4)(A),(G).) 
After a careful review of the facts you have provided, we decline to provide written 
advice to you at this time because the advice you have sought involves past conduct and there is 
a pending Enforcement action regarding the situation described in your request. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
Sincerely, 
Zackery P. Morazzini 
General Counsel 
l< 
By: 
Sukhi K. Brar 
Counsel, Legal Division 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through I 8997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

sections.conclusion: After a careful review of the facts you have provided, we decline to provide written advice to you at this time because the advice you have sought involves past conduct and there is a pending Enforcement action regarding the situation described in your request.

sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide written advice because the request involves past conduct and a pending enforcement action is already underway regarding the same situation.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Declination letter with explicit statement of reasons under Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A),(F) and (c)(4)(A),(G). No formal Q/C sections present, but conclusion clearly stated in body of letter.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declines to provide written advice to Mary Lopez regarding conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act, citing past conduct and a pending enforcement action as reasons for the declination.
